account_signals.author: unknown
account_signals.text:
I think ranges/horizontal levels are the most important factor when looking at pairs.

This is because even as value of an asset changes, relative value between two assets should stay the same so long as nothing unique to one of those assets changes. In other words, asset pairs should always trade in a range until something changes that is specific to one or both parts of the pair unequally.

Let's do an example with cars and houses. If an entry level car is $30k, then likely the current entry l

You're using a ±5°F tool to bet on a 2°F window. The math doesn't work regardless of your data source.

search_signals.text:
Six American service members are dead and the region is sliding toward a wider war, and you’re posting a paid betting line on whether the Strait of Hormuz closes.

This isn’t analysis. It’s war turned into a gambling market.

The Strait of Hormuz isn’t a Polymarket prop bet. It’s one of the most critical energy chokepoints on Earth. If it closes, global markets convulse, economies seize, and people die.

And you’re packaging it as a sponsored prediction market.

There is something profoundly bro

A stronger ISM Services PMI (above 50, esp. beating the ~53.5 consensus expected today) signals robust US economic activity and growth expectations. This is generally bullish for crypto (risk-on sentiment, equities lift), though it may cap Fed cut odds via stronger USD/yields. Bearish for gold (hurts safe-haven demand). Silver is mixed—growth aids industrial use but precious-metal flows often weigh. 

Weaker-than-expected data could flip it bullish for all three on higher rate-cut bets.

https://t.co/88FLfJuWnU doesn't really hand-pick projects to promote — it's mostly algorithmic. tokens that show up on trending and the front page get there through trading activity and volume.

the best thing you can do post-launch is keep your chart alive. consistent buys, active trading, and real volume signal to dexscreener and https://t.co/88FLfJuWnU that people care. a dead chart = nobody scrolling past it.

community helps too but the chart is what catches new eyes first.

search_signals.author: unknown
search_signals.text: nice branding. the tokens that actually survive on https://t.co/88FLfJuWnU are the ones that keep their chart alive post-launch. most people launch then just wait for buyers — but consistent volume is what gets you picked up by dexscreener scanners and onto trending pages where the real eyes are. early momentum is everything on solana right now
